metadata.title: Ministerial Order BA 2024 06 - Adaptable Dwelling Units, Part 9 Seismic, and General Updates
metadata.description: Revision 6 changes include updates to make adaptable dwelling units easier to design and construct, offering greater flexibility that can reduce space requirements and costs. The changes also clarify seismic requirements for houses and small buildings (Part 9) to support builder understanding and compliance. Additional general updates are also included.
metadata.authority: BC Ministry of Housing - Building and Safety Standards Branch
metadata.author: BC Building and Safety Standards Branch

content.text: sewage, water, electrical, telephone, rail or similar public infrastructure systems located in a street or a public transit right of way,

amendment.description: Amend Table 1.1.1.1.(5), Item 19 by striking out 'Ramps' and substituting 'Ramps', and by striking out '3.4.6.79.' and substituting '3.4.6.9.'

content.entry: Stairs, Ramps, Handrails and Guards ., ., ., Articles 3.4.6.5 to 3.4.6.9., .
Existing conditions that do not comply fully with the requirements are permitted if they are acceptable to the authority having jurisdiction.

revision.change-summary: Amended Table 1.1.1.1.(5), Item 19 by striking out italic "Ramps" and substituting regular "Ramps", and by striking out "3.4.6.79." and substituting "3.4.6.9."

amendment.description: Amend Table 1.1.1.1.(5), Item 37 by striking out term-referenced 'unsafe condition' and substituting plain text 'unsafe condition'

content.entry: Mechanical and Plumbing Systems Part 6 and Part 7

Existing mechanical and plumbing systems in buildings are not required to fully comply to the requirements of Parts 6 or Part 7 provided: (a) it is not an unsafe condition and (b) it is acceptable to the authority having jurisdiction.

revision.change-summary: Amended Table 1.1.1.1.(5), Item 37 by striking out term-referenced "unsafe condition" and substituting plain text "unsafe condition"

amendment.description: Amend Table 1.1.1.1.(6), Item 5 by striking out italic 'Ramps' and substituting regular 'Ramps', and by striking out italic 'ramp' and substituting regular 'ramp' throughout Item 5

content.entry:
Openings Near Unenclosed Exterior Exit Stairs and Ramps
Unprotected openings in exterior walls that are within 3 m horizontally and less than 10 m below or less than 5 m above an unenclosed exterior exit stair or ramp of a house with a secondary suite shall be protected where the unenclosed exterior exit stair or ramp provides the only means of egress from a suite and is exposed to fire from unprotected openings in the exterior walls of another dwelling unit, ancillary space or common space.
Protection of the unprotected openings as described in is not required when all smoke alarms within a house with a secondary suite are of photo-electric type and interconnected as described in .

revision.change-summary: Amended Table 1.1.1.1.(6), Item 5 by striking out italic "Ramps" and substituting regular "Ramps", and by striking out italic "ramp" and substituting regular "ramp" throughout Item 5

amendment.description: Amend Table 1.1.1.1.(6), Item 7 by striking out 'Fire-Resistance and Fire-Protection Ratings' and substituting 'Fire-Resistance Ratings and Fire-Protection Ratings'

content.entry:
Fire-Resistance Ratings and Fire-Protection Ratings
In a house with a secondary suite, where a minimum fire-resistance rating of 30 min is permitted, it is permitted to use wood-frame construction where stud and joist spaces are filled with absorptive material,resilient metal channel spaced 400 or 600 mm o.c. is on one side and not less than 12.7 mm thick gypsum board is installed on ceilings and on both sides of walls.

Adding resilient metal channel spaced 400 or 600 mm o.c. and an additional layer of not less than 12.7 mm gypsum board to one side of an existing finished wall assembly that has not less than 12.7 mm gypsum board on each side or an existing finished floor-ceiling assembly that has not less than 12.7 mm gypsum on the ceiling side is permitted to be used where a 30 min fire-resistance rating is required.

revision.change-summary: Amended Table 1.1.1.1.(6), Item 7 by striking out "Fire-Resistance and Fire-Protection Ratings" and substituting "Fire-Resistance Ratings and Fire-Protection Ratings"

content.definition:
means a review of the work
at a building site, and

amendment.description: Amend Notes to Part 1 Compliance, A-1.4.1.2.(1) Defined Terms by striking out '2018' in the fourth paragraph under 'Dangerous Goods'

revision.content: The British Columbia Building Code nomenclature takes a common sense approach that corresponds more closely to how people refer to dangerous goods on a daily basis, blending TDGR and WHMIS terminology without using nondescript numbers and letters as previously found in the British Columbia Building Code, TDGR and WHMIS.

clause.text: occupants live as a single housekeeping unit in a dwelling unit with sleeping accommodation for not more than 10 persons,
smoke alarms are installed in conformance with ,
emergency lighting is provided in conformance with , and
the building is sprinklered throughout.

amendment.description: Delete Notes to Part 3 Fire Protection, Occupant Safety and Accessibility, A-3.8.3.6.(3) Washrooms in Residential Occupancies in its entirety

Amend Notes to Part 3 Fire Protection, Occupant Safety and Accessibility, A-3.8.5.6.(1) Adaptable Dwelling Unit Floor Area by striking out second through fifth sentences and substituting new text, and adding additional paragraph

sentence.text: At least one bedroom or sleeping space in an adaptable dwelling unit shall have

clause.text: a floor area that permits a turning area of not less than 1 700 mm in diameter, or not less than 1 700 mm by 1 500 mm, that could be adjacent a bed,
a pathway clearance of not less than 850 mm wide, that could be unobstructed by a bed, to allow functional use of the bedroom, and
when provided, a closet with

subclause.text: a clear opening width of not less than 900 mm, and

a clear floor space, that need not be separate from the turning areas required in , of not less than 1 700 mm in diameter or 1 700 mm by 1 500 mm on at least one side of the closet. (See Note A-3.8.5.6.(1).)

sentence.text: Though the Code doesn't regulate bed furniture or sizes or styles such as bunkbeds, designs should allow for a bed to be located to accommodate transfer from the turning area to either side of the bed, such as by flipping the orientation of the bed, as well as for functional maneuvering space in the room.
A built-in closet is not required by this Code.

revision.change-summary: Struck out second through fifth sentences and substituted new text about bed furniture and built-in closets, and added additional paragraph

content.text: The seismic design parameter, Smax, is used as a trigger for the application of seismic design provisions in Part 9. It was derived by considering the upper limit on the minimum lateral earthquake force, V, as specified in , and is taken as the larger of 2/3 S(0.2) and S(0.5), with S(0.2) and S(0.5) calculated in accordance with .

amendment.description: Amend Notes to Part 9 Housing and Small Buildings, A-9.32.4.1. Naturally Aspirating Fuel-Fired Vented Appliance (NAFFVA) by striking out Tables A-9.32.4.1.(1)A and A-9.32.4.1.(1)B in their entirety, and substituting new tables

content.title: Naturally Aspirating Fuel-Fired Vented Appliance (NAFFVA)
content.paragraph: NAFFVA, typically appliances with draft hoods, are subject to back drafting when a negative pressure condition occurs in the dwelling. The following tables describe the conditions under which Sentence 9.32.4.1.(1) applies:

table.title: Table A-9.32.4.1.(1)A Vent Safety - Natural Gas and Propane

table-notes.note: (1) Any power vented appliance with pressurized vent (1 pipe) or sealed combustion (2 pipe) or direct vent appliance (fireplace, heater or HWT) are non-NAFFVA.
(2) Thermal buoyancy chimneys must be within the heated envelope of the house to provide acceptable venting performance.

(3) This category applies only to (a) mid-efficient forced air furnaces equipped with induced draft fans and exhaust proving switch, and (b) boilers equipped with induced draft fans and exhaust proving switch.
(4) Mid-efficient (draft induced) appliances are considered NAFFVA with the exception of a boiler or HWT located in an air-barriered room.

(5) Mechanical room must be air-barriered from remainder of house with no access from within house. Room must be lined with panel products with sealed joints and all pipe and wire penetrations sealed. Effectively, the room must be finished before equipment is installed and holes drilled for pipes and wires. This option is not available for forced air furnaces as it is not possible to effectively seal the ducts.

table.title: Table A-9.32.4.1.(1)B Vent Safety - Oil and Solid Fuel

row.entry: F/A Furnace Boiler HWT Fireplace Heat Stove

table-notes.note: (1) Thermal buoyancy chimneys must be within the heated envelope of the house to provide acceptable venting performance.

(2) Sealed combustion kits can be added to oil-fired appliances but they switch to interior combustion air if intake is blocked and rely on barometrically dampered thermal buoyancy chimneys so they are considered NAFFVA.
(3) Oil-fired HWT, boilers and furnaces equipped with blocked vent switches.

(4) Mechanical room must be air-barriered from remainder of house with no access from within house. Room must be lined with panel products with sealed joints and all pipe and wire penetrations sealed. Effectively, the room must be finished before equipment is installed and holes drilled for pipes and wires. This option is not available for forced air furnaces as it is not possible to effectively seal the ducts.
(5) Wood-burning appliances certified for use in mobile homes and installed to mobile home installation standards are considered non-NAFFVA and Sentence 9.32.4.1.(1) does not apply to them.

revision.change-summary: Replaced Tables A-9.32.4.1.(1)A and A-9.32.4.1.(1)B in their entirety with updated tables and notes

amendment.description: Amend Notes to Part 9 Housing and Small Buildings, A-9.36.1.3.(6) Exemptions (numbering) by striking out '9.36.1.3.(6)' and substituting '9.36.1.3.(5)'

content.title: Exemptions
content.paragraph: Examples of buildings and spaces that are exempted from the requirements of include seasonally heated buildings, storage and parking garages, small service buildings or service rooms and unconditioned spaces in buildings and unconditioned buildings such as storage warehouses. However, note that, where a building envelope assembly of an exempted building is adjacent to a conditioned space, this assembly must meet the requirements of .

revision.change-summary: Renumbered from 9.36.1.3.(6) to 9.36.1.3.(5)

amendment.description: Amend Appendix C, Climatic and Seismic Information for Building Design in Canada, by striking out 'for the 2010 edition of the Code' in the ninth paragraph under 'Wind Effects'

revision.content: As a result of the updating procedure, the 1-in-50 reference wind velocity pressures remained unchanged for most of the locations listed in . However, the reference wind velocity pressures for some locations were revised to reflect the availability of additional wind data. The reference wind velocity pressures for locations in British Columbia were revised to reflect the availability of additional wind data and the use of a different extreme value distribution.
revision.change-summary: Struck out "for the 2010 edition of the Code"
